_meta.purpose: Palette of the ACTUAL dashboard blocks captured 1:1 from the Figma 'Dashboard - Blocks' catalog (section 725:24527). Each block is a registry-valid Composition Plan fragment with <placeholders>. Assemble a dashboard by placing these following dashboard/example.json. Every `plan` uses ONLY registry-backed components, tokens and text styles.
_meta.source: Figma file uI6oKmJ0qDhsEuE0nFah2W, node 721:6689 (Dashboard - Blocks) → section 725:24527 (Dashboard Blocks).
_meta.placeholderConvention: <...> = fill with real content; optional trailing '?' means the node may be dropped when empty. A '<a|b|c>' placeholder is a closed choice.
_meta.refConvention: { "$ref": "block.id" } expands to that block's `plan` fragment. Composite blocks (rows/grids/lists) reference their item block. The agent expands $ref when authoring the Composition Plan — the render runtime never sees $ref.
_meta.capture: Blocks with `plan: null` are referenced by `source` node id but not yet renderable (e.g. the progress-bar / avatar data-table cell). Capture/extend before use — never approximate.
_meta.noPlaceholders: FILL every placeholder with real content, do not leave DS defaults. A Select MUST carry `text: { label, value }` (never 'Label'/'Input Text'). A functional Icon MUST carry a glyph `key` (from the DB Theme Icons library); an icon-only Button MUST carry an icon glyph via `applyProps`. If no real label/value/glyph/action applies, OMIT the node entirely rather than emit an empty ✕ / 'Input Text' placeholder.

dashboard.section-header.purpose: Section title + description block: an h2 Heading over a muted description. Opens a dashboard section. The runtime builds this automatically from a Section's `title`/`description`, so author it via those props rather than nesting this block manually.

dashboard.metric-card.purpose: Compact KPI card: a muted label, a large value Heading, and a Badge + short delta/trend note. Read-only, no action. Used in a 3-up metric row. (The reference card shows a decorative trailing arrow; it is omitted here because a generic Icon has no meaningful glyph — never render an empty placeholder icon.)

dashboard.metric-row.purpose: A row of 3 metric cards (auto equal-height grid). The typical first content block of a dashboard, directly under the section header.

dashboard.content-card.purpose: Rich content/teaser card: a title Heading, a description, a Badge + meta row, a Tag + meta row, and a single navigation Link. The whole card describes one entity (project, report, entry).

dashboard.table-row.purpose: A single data-table row: a Card wrapping a full-width Grid of text cells (4 columns here; the reference uses 6). Use for tabular records under a header row.

dashboard.data-table.purpose: Tabular data: a header row (bold column labels) followed by table rows. In the richest reference (Dashboard - Example) a cell may carry an avatar Image + text or an inline progress-bar (LoadingIndicator, Concept); those cell decorations are NOT yet exposed by the render runtime, so the text-cell form (dashboard.table-row) is the captured, renderable variant.

dashboard.toolbar.purpose: Filter toolbar row above a data table: Select filters, each carrying a real label + selected value. Only place it when concrete filters exist — fill EVERY Select's `text` with a real label + value; never leave the default 'Label'/'Input Text'. Trailing icon-button actions are optional and require a real glyph via `applyProps` (INSTANCE_SWAP icon key) — omit them if no glyph/action is defined (never an empty ✕ button).
